etymology.description: A person dancing with ox tails -> borrowed for 'have no'
etymology.interpretation: The character 無(wú, 'not') originally depicted a person holding ox tails, symbolizing a rain dance. It was borrowed to represent the abstract concept of absence or negation, while 舞(wǔ, 'dance') retained the original dancing meaning.

examples.chinese: 他无话可说(他無話可說)。
这个地方无危险(這個地方無危險)。
对于他的批评，我无可奉告(對於他的批評，我無可奉告)。
examples.pinyin: Duìyú tā de pīpíng, wǒ wú kě fèng gào.
examples.english: Regarding his criticism, I have no comment.
